title: dorycnium pentaphyllum var. candicans
intro: Dorycnium pentaphyllum var. candicans, also known as the White Broom, is a perennial legume plant native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, helping to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Dorycnium pentaphyllum var. candicans often involve infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
